summary_one_line: Input must carry the 4-byte ICC color-space signature 'HLS ' (0x48 4c 53 20) so cmsChannelsOfColorSpace dispatches into the cmsSigHlsData case.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A 4-byte FOURCC equal to the cmsSigHlsData signature 'HLS ' = 0x48 0x4c 0x53 0x20 at the profile color-space field (offset 0x10-0x13), which makes the switch on ColorSpace match case cmsSigHlsData at cmspcs.c:895 (true side).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every cmplog winner seed places the ASCII bytes 'HLS ' at offsets 0x10-0x13 (e.g. seed 66120690646875c5: 48 4c 53 20), and the per-role overlay shows L895 true side hit 14 times in W vs 0 in L.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser seed carries 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offsets 0x10-0x13 instead of 'HLS ', so the switch dispatches to case cmsSigRgbData (true 52 times in L) and never reaches the cmsSigHlsData arm.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the runtime constant 'HLS '/cmsSigHlsData (0x484c5320) was compared against the input color-space field, then substitutes that exact 4-byte operand at the matching input offset; naive has only havoc byte mutations and must blindly land all 4 magic bytes, which it never does (0/10 trials).

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the cmsSigHlsData switch case at cmspcs.c:895 inside cmsChannelsOfColorSpace.
Winner cmplog seeds place the ASCII bytes HLS at offsets 0x10-0x12.
Loser naive seeds carry RGB at offsets 0x10-0x12 instead of HLS.
Offsets 0x10/0x11/0x12 cleanly separate W (H/L/S) from L (R/G/B).
The cmsSigHlsData true side is hit in the winner role and never in the loser role.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 895  T=14 F=14  T=0 F=66  case cmsSigHlsData:

evidence_trail.claim: The loser instead dispatches into the cmsSigRgbData arm.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 893  T=10 F=18  T=52 F=14  case cmsSigRgbData:

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes a CMP operand at matching input offsets, which naive cannot do.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only havoc/token mutators and no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: naive blocked the branch in all 10 trials while cmplog resolved it in 8.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=8/10  blocked=2  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=0/10  blocked=10  unreached=0

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2295 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 2 --seed 66120690646875c5`; the chain has 9 levels and reports the I2S-floor signal with 2 ancestor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 7 and 8, direct lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace find in this cmplog winner's ancestry.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where naive resolves the cmsSigHlsData case at a rate comparable to cmplog despite the 4-byte 'HLS ' magic check, indicating the FOURCC gate is not the I2S-sensitive blocker.

weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row floor signal is a lower bound and the leaf mutation_op lists only havoc names, so the literal I2SRandReplace tag is not directly recorded on the winning seed itself.
